title: The sun is 27 million times heavier than the moon, yet its pull on our tides is only 46 per cent as strong — because tides answer to the difference in gravity across a planet, and distance kills that difference fast
description:
The Sun outweighs the Moon by a factor of 27 million, yet contributes only 46 percent of Earth's tidal force. The reason is a cube-law that punishes distance far more than gravity itself does.
The post The sun is 27 million times heavier than the moon, yet it…
content: Isaac Newton worked out the answer in Book III of the Principia in 1687, and the numbers still surprise anyone who meets them for the first time. The Sun weighs about 27 million times more than the M… [+8537 chars]
de:
Die Sonne ist 27 Millionen Mal schwerer als der Mond, doch ihre Anziehungskraft auf unsere Gezeiten ist nur 46 Prozent so stark – weil die Gezeiten auf den Unterschied in der Schwerkraft auf einem Planeten reagieren und die Entfernung diesen Unterschied schnell zunichte macht. Die Sonne übertrifft den Mond um den Faktor 27 Millionen, trägt jedoch nur 46 Prozent zur Gezeitenkraft der Erde bei. Der Grund ist ein Würfelgesetz, das die Entfernung weitaus stärker bestraft als die Schwerkraft selbst.
Der Beitrag Die Sonne ist 27 Millionen Mal schwerer als der Mond, und doch…
en:
The sun is 27 million times heavier than the moon, yet its pull on our tides is only 46 per cent as strong — because tides answer to the difference in gravity across a planet, and distance kills that difference fast. The Sun outweighs the Moon by a factor of 27 million, yet contributes only 46 percent of Earth's tidal force. The reason is a cube-law that punishes distance far more than gravity itself does.
The post The sun is 27 million times heavier than the moon, yet it…
la:
Sol 27 decies septies luna gravior est, sed tamen ad nostros aestus trahere tantum 46 centesimas tantum valet — quia aestus gravitatis per planetam differentiam respondent, et distantiam velocitatis differentiam necat. Sol praeponderat lunam per factorem 27 decies centena millia, tamen nonnisi XLVI centesimis viribus Thadal Terrae confert. Ratio cubus generum punit longe plus quam ipsa gravitas.
